content.explanation:
**Want — Present Tense में चाहना:**

"Want" एक stative verb है जो present tense में किसी चीज़ की इच्छा या अभिलाषा को व्यक्त करता है।

**Positive (सकारात्मक):**
- I/You/We/They + **want** + to + base verb / noun
- He/She/It + **wants** + to + base verb / noun

🇮🇳 मैं पानी पीना चाहता हूँ।
🇬🇧 I **want** to drink water.

🇮🇳 वह नई गाड़ी चाहती है।
🇬🇧 She **wants** a new car.

**Negative (नकारात्मक):**
- I/You/We/They + **do not want** + to + base verb / noun
- He/She/It + **does not want** + to + base verb / noun

🇮🇳 मैं यह नहीं चाहता।
🇬🇧 I **do not want** this.

🇮🇳 वह जाना नहीं चाहती।
🇬🇧 She **does not want** to go.

**Question (प्रश्नवाचक):**
- **Do** + I/you/we/they + **want** + to + base verb / noun?
- **Does** + he/she/it + **want** + to + base verb / noun?

🇮🇳 क्या तुम चाय पीना चाहते हो?
🇬🇧 **Do** you **want** to have tea?

🇮🇳 क्या वह आना चाहती है?
🇬🇧 **Does** she **want** to come?

**Want + Noun (Chaaiye structure):**
"Want" can be followed directly by a noun.
- I want water. (मुझे पानी चाहिए।)
- She wants a promotion. (उसे पदोन्नति चाहिए।)

**Want + Object + To + Verb:**
Subject + want/wants + object + to + base verb
- I want **him** to come. (मैं चाहता हूँ कि वह आए।)
- She wants **me** to help. (वह चाहती है कि मैं मदद करूँ।)
rules: I/You/We/They के साथ → want (कभी wants नहीं): I want, We want, They want.
He/She/It के साथ → wants (हमेशा s लगाएं): He wants, She wants, It wants.
Want + noun: Subject चाहता है कोई चीज़ — 'I want water', 'She wants a job'.
Want + to + base verb: Subject कुछ करना चाहता है — 'I want to go', 'He wants to study'.
Negative: I/You/We/They + do not want (या don't want); He/She/It + does not want (या doesn't want).
Question: Do + I/you/we/they + want...?; Does + he/she/it + want...? — Note: 'Does' के बाद want, न कि wants!
"Want" एक stative verb है — इसे continuous (-ing) form में कभी न लगाएं। 'I am wanting' WRONG है।
Want + object + to + verb pattern: 'I want him to come' — यह 'I want that he comes' नहीं होता।
Short answer: 'Yes, I do.' / 'No, she doesn't.' — 'does/do' को छोड़ें नहीं।
WH-questions: What do you want? Where does she want to go? Who wants to come?
content.memoryTrick:
**WANT = चाहना** — Simple rules to remember:
1. "I want + TO + verb" (action) → I want **to** go. ✅
2. "I want + noun" (thing) → I want **water**. ✅
3. He/She/It → always add **S** → He want**s**.
4. NEVER use "wanting" — it is a stative verb!
5. Does के बाद → base verb (Does he **want**, NOT Does he **wants**).

examples.hindi: वह एक अच्छी नौकरी चाहती है।
हम छुट्टी पर जाना चाहते हैं।
examples.english: We want to go on vacation.

examples.hindi: क्या तुम चाय पीना चाहते हो?
examples.english: Do you want to have tea?
He does not want to go.

examples.hindi: मेरी माँ स्वस्थ खाना बनाना चाहती हैं।
examples.english: My mother wants to cook healthy food.

examples.hindi: क्या आप कुछ और चाहते हैं?
वे कल तक प्रोजेक्ट खत्म करना चाहते हैं।
examples.english: They want to finish the project by tomorrow.

mistakes.why: "Want" एक stative verb है — इसे present continuous (-ing) में कभी use नहीं करते।
English में 'want that + clause' नहीं होता। Pattern है: want + object + to + verb।
Does के साथ हमेशा base verb (want) लगाएं, न कि wants।
They plural subject है — plural subjects के साथ 'want' (no s) लगाएं।
Want के बाद action के लिए 'to + base verb' ज़रूरी है — 'to' को ignore न करें।
She के लिए question में 'Do' नहीं, 'Does' लगाएं।
Double negative गलत है — 'don't want anything' या 'want nothing' (किसी एक का प्रयोग करें)।
Verb को proper conjugation चाहिए — 'wanting' alone as a finite verb incorrect है।
want + someone + to + base verb — subject pronoun (he) नहीं, object pronoun (him) लगाएं।

speakingTips: रोज़ सुबह एक sentence बोलो: 'Today I want to...' — इससे habit बनती है।
Family या दोस्तों से पूछो: 'Do you want to...?' — practical real-life practice।
Shopping या café में use करो: 'I want this in size medium, please' — इससे confidence बढ़ता है।
Negative form practice करो: 'I do not want to...' — conversation में यह बहुत ज़रूरी है।
Mirror के सामने practice करो — 'Does she want to come?' जैसे questions ज़ोर से बोलो।
Office में बोलो: 'I want to discuss this topic.' — professional English के लिए यह pattern बहुत useful है।
हर new word के साथ want का sentence बनाओ — जैसे 'I want to achieve my goals.' — vocabulary + grammar एक साथ।
Contractions use करो natural speech में: 'I don't want to...' sounds much more natural than 'I do not want to...' in everyday conversation.

essay.title: My Goals for the Future
essay.hindi: यह निबंध एक विद्यार्थी के भविष्य के लक्ष्यों के बारे में है जो 'want' क्रिया का प्रयोग करते हुए अपनी इच्छाएँ व्यक्त करता है।
essay.english:
Every person has dreams and goals. I want to build a bright future for myself and my family. First, I want to complete my studies with good marks. My teacher wants all students to work hard and never give up. My parents want me to become a successful professional.

I want to improve my English every single day. I do not want to make the same mistakes again and again. My friend also wants to learn English with me, and we help each other. She wants to speak confidently in every situation.

In my career, I want to get a good job at a reputed company. I want to contribute to the team and grow as a professional. I do not want to stay in one place — I want to keep learning new skills. Does the company want people who are ready to work hard? Yes, every company wants dedicated employees.

My mother wants me to stay healthy while I work towards my goals. She wants me to eat well and exercise daily. My father wants me to save money wisely. Together, my family wants to see me succeed.

In short, I want to be happy, healthy, and successful. I want to make my parents proud, and I want to inspire others who are on the same journey.

essay.title: A Good Employee
essay.hindi: यह निबंध एक अच्छे कर्मचारी के गुणों पर है, जिसमें 'want' का प्रयोग करते हुए बताया गया है कि एक अच्छा कर्मचारी क्या चाहता है और नियोक्ता क्या चाहते हैं।
essay.english:
Every company wants good employees. But what does a good employee actually look like? A good employee wants to learn every day. He or she does not want to stay in the comfort zone. They want to take on new challenges with a positive attitude.

A good employee wants to communicate clearly with the team. He does not want to hide problems — he wants to solve them. The manager wants employees who speak up when they see a mistake. The team wants honest and open communication.

Does a good employee want only a high salary? No! A good employee wants to add real value to the company. She wants to complete her tasks on time and help others when needed. She does not want to miss deadlines.

The company also wants to give back to its employees. It wants to provide training, growth opportunities, and a healthy work environment. Every worker wants to feel respected and appreciated.

In an interview, an employer wants to know what you want from the job and what you can offer. You want to show your skills, your attitude, and your goals clearly. Both sides want to find the right match.

In the end, a great workplace is built when both the company and its employees want the same thing — success, respect, and growth.

essay.title: Health is Wealth
essay.hindi: यह निबंध स्वास्थ्य के महत्व पर है, जिसमें रोज़मर्रा की इच्छाओं को 'want' का उपयोग करते हुए व्यक्त किया गया है।
essay.english:
Everyone wants to be healthy. Good health is the foundation of a happy life. I want to take care of my body every day. I want to eat healthy food, exercise regularly, and sleep on time. My doctor wants me to drink at least eight glasses of water daily.

My mother wants the whole family to eat home-cooked meals. She does not want us to eat junk food every day. My father wants to go for a morning walk, but sometimes he does not want to wake up early. We all want good health, but we need discipline to achieve it.

Do you want to feel energetic and fresh every day? Then you need to exercise. It does not have to be hard. I just want to walk for thirty minutes each morning. My sister wants to join a yoga class. My friend wants to start swimming.

Mental health is also important. I want to stay positive and calm. I do not want to feel stressed about small things. My teacher wants students to meditate for a few minutes each morning. Does meditation really help? Many people want to try it and see the results.

In conclusion, I want to make health a priority. The body is a gift, and I want to protect it. A healthy person can achieve more, dream bigger, and live fully.

essay.title: My Dream Job
essay.hindi: यह निबंध एक सपनों की नौकरी पर है जिसमें विद्यार्थी 'want' का प्रयोग करके अपनी व्यावसायिक इच्छाएँ बताता है।
essay.english:
Everyone wants a job that makes them happy. I want a career where I can use my skills, help people, and grow every day. My dream job is in the field of technology. I want to become a software developer.

Why do I want this career? I want to create useful applications that solve real problems. I want to work with a talented team. My friend also wants to join the tech industry. She wants to become a data analyst. We both want to study hard and get the right qualifications.

In my dream job, I do not want to just sit and wait for instructions. I want to take initiative and bring new ideas. My future employer will probably want someone who is proactive and creative. I want to show those qualities from day one.

Does the company want experience? Yes, most companies want candidates with some experience. That is why I want to do internships while I study. I want to learn from real projects and real people.

I also want a job that offers work-life balance. I do not want to work eighteen hours a day. I want time for my family, my health, and my hobbies. A good company understands this and wants its employees to be happy.

In the end, I want to love my work. I want to wake up every morning excited to go to the office. That is my dream.

essay.title: Traveling: Why I Want to See the World
essay.hindi: यह निबंध यात्रा के प्रति प्रेम पर है, जिसमें 'want' का उपयोग करते हुए लेखक अपनी यात्रा की इच्छाएँ और कारण बताता है।
essay.english:
I want to travel the world. Traveling is one of the best ways to learn about life, people, and cultures. Every time I read about a new country, I want to go there and experience it myself.

First, I want to visit the mountains. I want to see the snow-covered peaks and breathe fresh air. My friend does not want to go to the mountains — she wants to visit the beach instead. She wants to swim in the ocean and watch the sunset. We both want different things, and that is perfectly fine.

I also want to meet people from other countries. I want to listen to their stories and understand their way of life. Traveling teaches us that we are all different, but we all want the same basic things — love, peace, and happiness.

Does traveling cost a lot of money? Yes, it can. That is why I want to save money every month for my travel fund. I do not want to spend all my money at once — I want to plan carefully and travel smartly.

My mother does not want me to travel alone. She wants me to go with a friend or join a group. I understand her concern, and I want to be safe too.

In summary, I want to explore the world, not just see it on a screen. I want to create memories, learn new languages, and grow as a person. Traveling is not just a hobby — it is an education.

story.title: Ravi's Big Decision
story.english:
Ravi is a young man who wants to change his life. He works at a small shop, but he wants more for himself. He wants to study and get a better job.

One day, Ravi tells his mother about his plans. She wants him to be happy. "Do you want to go back to school?" she asks. "Yes, I want to study and become an accountant," he says.

His father does not want him to stop working. "We need money," he says. But his mother does not want Ravi to give up his dream. She wants him to try.

Ravi decides to study at night. He wants to work during the day and study in the evenings. It is hard, but he does not want to quit. His sister wants to help him. She buys him books and helps him practice English.

After two years, Ravi passes his exams. He wants to celebrate with his family. His father smiles and says, "I am proud of you." Ravi wants to thank his mother and sister. They all sit together and eat a big meal.

Ravi gets a job at an accounting firm. He wants to work hard and grow. He is happy. He wanted a better life, and now he has one.
story.hindi:
रवि एक युवा लड़का है जो अपनी ज़िंदगी बदलना चाहता है। वह एक छोटी दुकान पर काम करता है, लेकिन वह अपने लिए कुछ बेहतर चाहता है। वह पढ़ना और एक अच्छी नौकरी पाना चाहता है।

एक दिन, रवि अपनी माँ को अपने इरादों के बारे में बताता है। वह उसे खुश देखना चाहती हैं। "क्या तुम वापस स्कूल जाना चाहते हो?" वह पूछती हैं। "हाँ, मैं पढ़कर अकाउंटेंट बनना चाहता हूँ," वह कहता है।

उसके पिता नहीं चाहते कि वह काम छोड़े। "हमें पैसों की ज़रूरत है," वे कहते हैं। लेकिन उसकी माँ नहीं चाहतीं कि रवि अपना सपना छोड़े। वह चाहती हैं कि वह कोशिश करे।

रवि रात को पढ़ने का फैसला करता है। वह दिन में काम और शाम को पढ़ाई करना चाहता है। यह कठिन है, लेकिन वह हार नहीं मानना चाहता। उसकी बहन उसकी मदद करना चाहती है। वह उसके लिए किताबें खरीदती है।

दो साल बाद, रवि परीक्षा पास कर लेता है। वह परिवार के साथ जश्न मनाना चाहता है। उसके पिताजी मुस्कुराते हैं। रवि को एक अकाउंटिंग फर्म में नौकरी मिल जाती है। वह खुश है।

story.title: The Interview
story.english:
Priya wants to get a job at a big company. She wants to prepare well for her interview. She wants to speak confidently and make a good impression.

The night before the interview, her mother asks, "Do you want me to help you practice?" Priya smiles. "Yes, I want to practice answering questions," she says.

Her mother plays the role of the interviewer. "What do you want from this job?" she asks. Priya thinks carefully. "I want to grow professionally and contribute to the team," she answers.

The next morning, Priya does not want to be late. She wakes up early, dresses professionally, and takes the bus to the office. She wants to look confident.

At the company, the interviewer asks many questions. "Does this role offer what you want?" she asks. "Yes," says Priya. "I want to work in an innovative environment and I want to learn from the best in the industry."

After the interview, Priya feels good. She does not want to check her phone constantly — she wants to stay calm and wait. Two days later, she gets a call. She got the job! She wants to call her mother immediately. "We did it!" she says. Her mother laughs. "No — you did it."
story.hindi:
प्रिया एक बड़ी कंपनी में नौकरी पाना चाहती है। वह इंटरव्यू की अच्छी तैयारी करना चाहती है। वह आत्मविश्वास से बोलना और अच्छा प्रभाव डालना चाहती है।

इंटरव्यू से पहले की रात, उसकी माँ पूछती हैं, "क्या तुम चाहती हो कि मैं तुम्हारी practice में मदद करूँ?" प्रिया मुस्कुराती है। "हाँ, मैं सवालों के जवाब देने की practice करना चाहती हूँ।"

उसकी माँ इंटरव्यूअर की भूमिका निभाती हैं। "तुम इस नौकरी से क्या चाहती हो?" प्रिया सोचती है। "मैं पेशेवर रूप से बढ़ना चाहती हूँ और टीम में योगदान देना चाहती हूँ।"

अगली सुबह, प्रिया देर नहीं करना चाहती। वह अच्छे कपड़े पहनती है और office जाती है। वह confident दिखना चाहती है।

इंटरव्यू में अच्छे सवाल होते हैं। प्रिया आत्मविश्वास से जवाब देती है। दो दिन बाद उसे फ़ोन आता है — नौकरी मिल गई! वह तुरंत माँ को बताना चाहती है। "हमने कर दिखाया!" "नहीं," माँ हँसती हैं, "तुमने कर दिखाया।"

story.title: A Family Outing
story.english:
On Sunday morning, everyone in the Sharma family wants to do something different. Raj wants to go to the park. His sister Meena wants to visit the shopping mall. Their mother does not want to go outside — she wants to rest at home. Their father wants to try a new restaurant for lunch.

"Do we all want to do different things?" asks the father with a smile. Everyone laughs.

They decide to make a plan. First, Raj and Meena go to the park. They want to play for one hour. Then, they all want to go to the restaurant together for lunch. After lunch, the mother does not want to shop, but she wants to sit in a café while the children and father look around the mall.

At the park, Raj runs and shouts, "I want to stay here all day!" But Meena does not want to stay. She wants to move on.

At the restaurant, the father orders food. "Does anyone want extra spice?" he asks. Raj raises his hand. The mother does not want spicy food today.

After a wonderful afternoon, they head home. "Did you enjoy?" asks the mother. "Yes!" everyone says together. Even the mother smiles. Sometimes, doing different things together is what a family wants most.
story.hindi:
रविवार की सुबह, शर्मा परिवार के सभी लोग कुछ अलग-अलग करना चाहते हैं। राज पार्क जाना चाहता है। उसकी बहन मीना शॉपिंग मॉल जाना चाहती है। उनकी माँ बाहर नहीं जाना चाहतीं — वह घर पर आराम करना चाहती हैं। उनके पिता एक नए रेस्टोरेंट में लंच करना चाहते हैं।

"क्या हम सब अलग-अलग चीज़ें करना चाहते हैं?" पिताजी मुस्कुराते हुए पूछते हैं। सब हँसते हैं।

vे एक योजना बनाते हैं। पहले राज और मीना पार्क जाते हैं। फिर सब साथ मिलकर रेस्टोरेंट जाते हैं। खाने के बाद बच्चे और पिता मॉल में घूमते हैं, जबकि माँ कैफे में बैठना चाहती हैं।

पार्क में राज चिल्लाता है, "मैं यहाँ पूरा दिन रहना चाहता हूँ!" लेकिन मीना नहीं रहना चाहती। रेस्टोरेंट में पिता पूछते हैं, "किसे ज़्यादा मसाला चाहिए?" राज हाथ उठाता है। माँ आज तीखा नहीं चाहतीं।

शाम को घर लौटते हुए सब खुश हैं। माँ भी मुस्कुराती हैं। कभी-कभी साथ मिलकर अलग-अलग चीज़ें करना ही परिवार के लिए सबसे अच्छा होता है।

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: सुप्रभात! तुम आज कितनी जल्दी आ गए।
turns.english: Good morning! You came so early today.

turns.speaker: Anita
turns.hindi: हाँ, मैं आज अपनी report जल्दी खत्म करना चाहती हूँ।
turns.english: Yes, I want to finish my report early today.

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: अच्छा। क्या तुम team meeting में join करना चाहती हो?
turns.english: Nice. Do you want to join the team meeting at ten?

turns.speaker: Anita
turns.hindi: ज़रूर। मैं उस project पर अपने ideas share करना चाहती हूँ।
turns.english: Definitely. I want to share my ideas on that project.

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: मैनेजर एक नई strategy discuss करना चाहते हैं।
turns.english: The manager wants to discuss a new strategy.

turns.speaker: Anita
turns.hindi: ओह, क्या वह budget भी review करना चाहते हैं?
turns.english: Oh, does he want to review the budget too?

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: हाँ। वह हम सभी से feedback चाहते हैं।
turns.english: Yes. He wants feedback from all of us.

turns.speaker: Anita
turns.hindi: ठीक है। मैं अपने notes तैयार करना चाहती हूँ।
turns.english: Alright. I want to prepare my notes before the meeting.

turns.speaker: Rahul
turns.hindi: क्या तुम पहले coffee पीना चाहती हो?
turns.english: Do you want to have a coffee first?

turns.speaker: Anita
turns.hindi: हाँ, बिल्कुल! मुझे एक कप कॉफ़ी चाहिए।
turns.english: Yes, absolutely! I want a cup of coffee.

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: बताइए, आप यह नौकरी क्यों करना चाहते हैं?
turns.english: Tell me, why do you want this job?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: मैं एक ऐसी कंपनी में काम करना चाहता हूँ जहाँ मैं बढ़ सकूँ।
turns.english: I want to work at a company where I can grow.

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: आप अपने करियर में क्या हासिल करना चाहते हैं?
turns.english: What do you want to achieve in your career?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: मैं एक senior professional बनना चाहता हूँ और team lead करना चाहता हूँ।
turns.english: I want to become a senior professional and I want to lead a team.

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: क्या आप अतिरिक्त घंटे काम करना चाहते हैं?
turns.english: Do you want to work extra hours when required?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: हाँ, मैं ज़रूरत पड़ने पर अतिरिक्त समय देना चाहता हूँ।
turns.english: Yes, I want to put in extra time when it is needed.

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: आप अपनी salary के बारे में क्या चाहते हैं?
turns.english: What do you want in terms of salary?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: मैं industry के standard के अनुसार salary चाहता हूँ।
turns.english: I want a salary that matches the industry standard.

turns.speaker: Interviewer
turns.hindi: हम आपको offer देना चाहते हैं। क्या आप joining date confirm करना चाहते हैं?
turns.english: We want to make you an offer. Do you want to confirm a joining date?

turns.speaker: Candidate
turns.hindi: जी, मैं यह offer accept करना चाहता हूँ। बहुत-बहुत धन्यवाद!
turns.english: Yes, I want to accept this offer. Thank you very much!

turns.speaker: Mother
turns.hindi: बेटा, तुम आज रात क्या खाना चाहते हो?
turns.english: Son, what do you want to eat tonight?

turns.speaker: Rohan
turns.hindi: माँ, मुझे पास्ता चाहिए। क्या आप बना सकती हैं?
turns.english: Mom, I want pasta. Can you make it?

turns.speaker: Mother
turns.hindi: हाँ, लेकिन तुम्हारी बहन दाल-चावल खाना चाहती है।
turns.english: Yes, but your sister wants to eat dal and rice.

turns.speaker: Rohan
turns.hindi: ठीक है, मैं भी दाल-चावल खाना चाहता हूँ।
turns.english: Okay, I also want to have dal and rice then.

turns.speaker: Father
turns.hindi: क्या सब TV देखना चाहते हैं खाने के बाद?
turns.english: Do all of you want to watch TV after dinner?

turns.speaker: Sister
turns.hindi: नहीं पापा, मैं अपनी homework करना चाहती हूँ।
turns.english: No Dad, I want to do my homework.

turns.speaker: Father
turns.hindi: बहुत अच्छा! मैं भी तुम्हारी मदद करना चाहता हूँ।
turns.english: Very good! I want to help you too.

turns.speaker: Mother
turns.hindi: और Rohan, क्या तुम आज रात जल्दी सोना चाहते हो?
turns.english: And Rohan, do you want to sleep early tonight?

turns.speaker: Rohan
turns.hindi: हाँ माँ। मुझे कल का exam देना है, मैं आराम करना चाहता हूँ।
turns.english: Yes Mom. I want to rest because I have an exam tomorrow.

turns.speaker: Mother
turns.hindi: ठीक है बेटा। हम सब चाहते हैं कि तुम अच्छे नंबर लाओ।
turns.english: Alright son. We all want you to get good marks.

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: नमस्ते! आप क्या खाना चाहते हैं?
turns.english: Hello! What do you want to eat?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.hindi: मुझे पहले menu देखना है।
turns.english: I want to see the menu first.

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: बिल्कुल। क्या आप कोई drink लेना चाहते हैं?
turns.english: Of course. Do you want to order a drink?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.hindi: हाँ, मुझे एक गिलास ठंडा पानी चाहिए।
turns.english: Yes, I want a glass of cold water please.

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: ज़रूर। और आपके साथ जो हैं, क्या वे भी order करना चाहते हैं?
turns.english: Sure. Does your companion also want to order?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.hindi: हाँ, वह vegetarian खाना चाहती है।
turns.english: Yes, she wants vegetarian food.

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: हमारे पास बहुत अच्छे vegetarian options हैं। क्या आप तीखा खाना चाहती हैं?
turns.english: We have great vegetarian options. Do you want something spicy?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.hindi: मैं medium spicy चाहती हूँ, लेकिन मेरी दोस्त ज़्यादा तीखा नहीं चाहती।
turns.english: I want medium spicy, but my friend does not want it very hot.

turns.speaker: Waiter
turns.hindi: ठीक है। क्या आप dessert भी लेना चाहती हैं?
turns.english: Understood. Do you want to have dessert as well?

turns.speaker: Kavya
turns.hindi: हाँ, मैं गुलाब जामुन चाहती हूँ। मेरी दोस्त ice cream लेना चाहती है।
turns.english: Yes, I want gulab jamun. My friend wants to have ice cream.
